methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $529M - $2.6B.

data.narrative:
Oxford Nanopore Technologies is a public-stage biotech company operating in the Biotech & Health sector. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 3 public peers and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage: Freenome ($2.6B), Recursion Pharmaceuticals ($1.8B), and Alto Neuroscience ($529M).

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach. The median valuation across the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables is $1.8B, with a range spanning $529M to $2.6B. This method captures the central tendency of publicly traded biotech firms with similar technological and commercial profiles. A public-comp anchoring approach using sector P/S multiples (6.1x median across RXRX, TEM, and ONT.L) cannot be applied, as the target's revenue figure is not on file.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $1.5B to $2.1B, with a median of $1.8B. This range is derived from the peer-median method and reflects the observed valuation dispersion within the comparable set. Key uncertainties include the absence of revenue data, which precludes multiple-based corroboration, and the relatively narrow peer sample (n=3), which limits robustness of the median estimate. Investors should monitor the width of the peer range ($529M to $2.6B) as indicative of valuation volatility within this sector and stage.
